events.text: Record Q3 FY26 print: revenue US$1.8B (+7% q/q, +21% y/y reported; +9% q/q, +27% y/y pro forma); non-GAAP GM 39.6% (+57bps q/q); op margin 20.3%; non-GAAP EPS US$1.41 (+55% y/y). DC&Comms +>40% y/y; communications +60% y/y on DCI/scale-across. Record backlog, orders out to CY2028 and LTAs to the end of the decade.

Anderson called the June quarter "a new inflection point" and reiterated FY27 growth > FY26. Demand "exceptionally strong with no signs of attenuation", visibility into CY2028, multi-year LTAs that combine upfront customer capex + demand commitments.

Q4 FY26 guide: revenue US$1.91–2.05B, GM 39–41%, non-GAAP EPS US$1.52–1.72 — implied acceleration. 6-inch InP pulled forward: internal capacity doubles by year-end (one quarter early), then >2x again by CY27; OCS TAM raised to >US$4B; CPO TAM >US$15B.

events.src: COHR · Q3 FY26 earnings call (O'Malley/Barclays Q&A)
events.text: Gross-margin fall-through seen as disappointing given the 6-inch ramp and exits — incremental GM only ~44% y/y; "margins leave more to be desired" (O'Malley/Barclays question).

MS sees Coherent as less in control of its own capacity than Lumentum — "it's just taken a little bit longer to ramp". MS preference order: Lumentum > Corning > Coherent > Ciena. EW PT raised to US$330. Space trades ~25x CY28 EPS post-NVDA.

JPM: Coherent guiding a quarter ahead on the 2x InP ramp for cal-26, then another 2x in cal-27; high-teens data-center revenue growth message in the June quarter, no pushback on acceleration to ~30%+ in FY27; 6-inch becomes >50% of InP volume from the September quarter, when it contributes materially to GM.

Coherent cited among Wolfspeed's SiC power-semi peers (Infineon, Mitsubishi, Navitas, onsemi, PI, ROHM, STMicro) in the 800V wide-bandgap opportunity (WBG US$1bn→US$5bn+ by 2030) — a peripheral optionality mention; the equity story remains optics.
Stock fell ~11% on 2026-06-10 on CPO-delay concerns (JPM framed it as overdone).

Coherent affirms a path to >42% GM via 1.6T mix, 6-inch wafers, more internal sourcing and industrial pricing. Every product on 6-inch (EMLs, CW lasers, photodiodes) yields better than the prior generation; demand "quite strong" with limited double-ordering; likely NVDA ELS outcome = Coherent + Fabrinet as the two suppliers.
InP substrate is a structural China supply risk — the CEO reportedly traveled to China with a Trump delegation seeking InP. FinTwit treats COHR as a "hold + accumulate" durable winner.
JPM investor meetings in NYC (Anderson + IR Silverstein) — management access post-selloff, read-through to sentiment.

Expert (Bob): in CPO the bottleneck is the ultra-high-power laser — only Coherent and Lumentum are a reliable source (one external laser feeds multiple ports → higher ASP, 'essentially two, possibly three' suppliers). In coherent/scale-across (800ZR), captives Cisco-Acacia/Nokia/Ciena supply modules; MRVL supplies DSPs. Most early CPO designs use external laser sources — reinforces the COHR/LITE moat on the UHP light source.

Engineering-driven note re-affirms Lumentum's UHP laser as superior and calls Coherent's UHP laser 'not viable' — a dissent from the 'COHR + LITE both reliable UHP sources' consensus (BTG 06-25, FundaAI 06-28). A single-analyst opinion, but a direct competitive-quality challenge to the COHR light-source moat worth tracking against the qualification-history bull case.

'Bringing More Light to Scale-Up Networks' — scale-up market ~$70bn by 2030 (>4x prior est); copper stays longer, CPO eventual (meaningful at NVDA Feynman — small 2028, stronger 2029+), optical sell-offs 'overdone'. COHR a 'clearest beneficiary' of optical into scale-up (with LITE/GLW), broad exposure across the full CPO/NPO stack (CW lasers, ELS modules, FAUs, PM fiber, isolators, TECs); mgmt 'the most aggressive on scale-up sizing — orders of magnitude larger than scale-out'. Model: 2027E rev $9,438M, 2028E rev $12,863M (+2% vs prior); FY28 EPS sensitivity ~$10.8-$12.65.

'InP Supply Constraint Will Likely Persist' — Buy/PT $420 reiterated (price $299.38); pullback = buying opportunity. Debuts an InP (EML+CW) supply model: laser-chip supply ~280M (2025) → ~1.1B (2028E, ~+60% CAGR); 6-inch the biggest lever (4.36x more chips/wafer). EML ~33% below demand 2026 → 20%/11% (2027/28); CW gap widens to 19%/55% (2027/28) on CPO/NPO high-power demand. COHR = the 6-inch leader and most-internal supplier vs China; the CPO-TAM swing is whether AVGO buys CPO components from COHR or self-integrates.

cells: 1.6T ramps CY25; showed 3 laser variants at OFC
DC +23% y/y but InP-constrained; record bookings
DC +37% y/y, +13% q/q; 1.6T ramp "faster than expected"
InP output tripled y/y; CHIPS funds Sherman
6-inch to start next qtr; capacity +3x y/y
At 80% of doubling target; on track by Dec
Doubling 1 qtr early; >2x again by CY27; 3rd site Zurich
OCS revenue expected CY25; NVIDIA CPO collab
OCS to 7 customers; CPO CW sampling
OCS TAM >$4B; CPO TAM >$15B; NVIDIA $2B deal
